headline: Israel has damaged or destroyed 45% of towns in southern Lebanon
description: According to an exclusive analysis of radar satellite images conducted by Le Monde and Masae Analytics, nearly half of the urban centers in southern Lebanon have been affected or even destroyed since the beginning of Israel's ground offensive in March 2026.

headline: Macron opens the door to 'reparations' for slavery
Ebola in the DRC: 'The epidemic is unprecedented in scale'
War in Ukraine: Kherson and Oleshky, two different worlds just 10 kilometers apart
description: On the front line, the small town of Oleshky in the south has been occupied by the Russian military since the invasion began. Residents try to reach Kherson at great personal risk.

headline: 'In a country as polarized as Spain, it is Pedro Sánchez's international stance that garners the most support'
description: The Spanish prime minister's positions on Donald Trump's policies and the situation in the Middle East have drawn fierce criticism from foreign leaders. Yet Sánchez's assertiveness on the world stage stands in sharp contrast to his weakness at home, explains writer Daniel Gascón.

headline: SpaceX postpones highly anticipated Starship launch
description: After several rounds of stopping and starting the countdown clock, the company said engineers would not be able to work through last-minute glitches in time to lift off Thursday. SpaceX is now targeting Friday for another take-off attempt. The trial mission comes amid high stakes for the space company eyeing a blockbuster initial public offering.

headline: Canal+ 'blacklist': Over half of recent French films credit people now threatened with retribution
description: The CEO of Canal+ has threatened to boycott film industry figures who signed an open letter against the company's owner, Vincent Bolloré. Together, the signatories are credited in over 600 films produced in France over the past five years.

Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
description: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

headline: In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
description: France is experiencing a record number of data breaches. Despite the damage caused by these hacks, the level of cybersecurity in businesses and government agencies remains inadequate.

headline: JR covers Pont-Neuf bridge in Paris with giant inflatable cave
description: The temporary installation 'La Caverne du Pont-Neuf' has been set up on the Paris landmark since Thursday. The site will be open to the public free of charge, 24 hours a day, for three weeks from June 6.
